body:
Fixes #796.

Two independent root causes — both **false positives** — landed on merge commit `12a4b34e` and together produced what looked like one broad CI collapse.

Full investigation, logs and evidence: [`dev/log/issues/796/pulls/797/`](dev/log/issues/796/pulls/797/).

## Root cause A — version-pinned npm deprecation allowlist

Every job that installs Node dependencies failed with exit code 1 *after* npm itself succeeded (`added 587 packages in 21s`):

```
Unexpected npm stderr; update dependencies or explicitly classify the diagnostic:
npm warn deprecated glob@10.5.0: Old versions of glob are not supported, ...
##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.
```

`scripts/install-node-dependencies.sh` allowlisted reviewed upstream deprecations by **exact `name@version`** (`*"npm warn deprecated glob@7.2.3:"*`). Transitive versions float without any change on our side; when `archiver-utils` resolved `glob` to `10.5.0` the warning no longer matched, fell through to the "unexpected" bucket, and failed the `.vsix` packaging job **and all four desktop builds**.

**Corrected attribution.** The old allowlist implied `glob` came from electron-builder or vsce. Verified against the committed lockfiles, it does not:

```
@link-assistant/web-capture@^1.10.10 -> archiver@7.0.1
  -> archiver-utils@5.0.2 (glob ^10.0.0) -> glob@10.5.0
```

`@vscode/vsce@3.9.2` already uses `glob ^13.0.6`; electron-builder pulls only `glob@7.2.3`. Upstream deprecated every glob below 12.x — including the releases that *fix* CVE-2025-64756 ([isaacs/node-glob#644](https://github.com/isaacs/node-glob/issues/644)) — so the warning is unavoidable noise, not an actionable vulnerability.

**Fix.** Reviewed deprecations are matched by **package name only**, with accurate per-package tracking URLs. Unreviewed diagnostics still fail the build, so the check keeps its value.

## Root cause B — desktop builds

Not independent: all `Build <platform>` jobs and the `vscode` job fail through the same shared script, so fix A resolves them all.

## Root cause C — a blank line hides a git trailer

Auto Release failed immediately after computing version `0.299.0`:

```
Error recording self-hosting release metric: commit 59650f2b...
must record both Formal-AI-Session and Formal-AI-Evidence
```

**The commit records both.** But a blank line sits between them. Per [git-interpret-trailers](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-interpret-trailers), trailers are extracted from *one* group that *"must either be at the end of the input or be the last non-whitespace lines before a line that starts with `---`."* The blank line splits them into two paragraphs, so `%(trailers:key=Formal-AI-Session)` returned nothing — a **false negative** in trailer detection escalating into a failed release.

**Fix.** `trailer_values` now scans the full commit body for `Key: value` lines, case-insensitively. Placement no longer decides whether a compliant commit is recognised.

## How to reproduce

```bash
# Root cause C, in a clean fixture:
git commit -m "subject

Formal-AI-Session: ses_abc

Formal-AI-Evidence: docs/e.log"
git show -s '--format=%(trailers:key=Formal-AI-Session,valueonly)' HEAD   # -> empty
```

## Tests

- `tests/unit/ci-cd/issue_796.rs` — five tests driving the **real script** against a fake `npm`: the floated `glob@10.5.0` line no longer fails; unreviewed diagnostics still do; scoped names keep their scope; verbose mode exists and is off by default; the allowlist may not contain a pinned version.
- `tests/unit/specification/self_hosting_metric.rs` — a commit whose trailers are separated by a blank line is attributed correctly.

Both suites were **confirmed to fail against the pre-fix scripts** and pass after; the trailer test reproduces the production error string verbatim. `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` and `cargo fmt --check` are clean; all 111 `ci_cd::` tests pass, including the pre-existing `issue_730` assertions on the modified script.

## Debug output

`INSTALL_NODE_DEPENDENCIES_VERBOSE=1` traces every classification decision, so an unexpected diagnostic can be diagnosed from CI logs without a local reproduction. **Off by default**, enforced by test.

## Scope notes

- The two `skipped` Desktop Release runs listed in the issue are **benign** — `workflow_run` runs whose `resolve` job found no desktop assets were needed. No change required.
- Verified both defective patterns exist in exactly one place each, and that every workflow npm install routes through the shared script, so the fixes apply repo-wide.
- The template comparison (issue requirement) is in [`template-gap-analysis.md`](dev/log/issues/796/pulls/797/template-gap-analysis.md). It found six real gaps (missing `Cargo.lock` guard job, no post-publish smoke test, no secrets scanning, no fresh-merge simulation, non-resilient Buildx setup, file-size check excluding `.yml`/`.md`) plus five fixes this repo has that the templates lack. Those are **deliberately not bundled here** — they are unrelated to the #796 failures and each deserves separate review. Recommended landing order is documented in that file.

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/796

Both defects landed on merge commit 12a4b34e and together failed the
Desktop Release and CI/CD Pipeline workflows (issue #796).

scripts/install-node-dependencies.sh matched reviewed npm deprecation
warnings by exact name@version. Transitive versions float without any
change on our side, so when archiver-utils resolved glob from 7.2.3 to
10.5.0 the warning stopped matching, fell through to the "unexpected"
bucket, and failed the .vsix job plus every desktop build even though
npm install itself succeeded. Reviewed deprecations are now matched by
package name only, with accurate per-package tracking URLs: glob reaches
both workspaces via @link-assistant/web-capture -> archiver ->
archiver-utils, not via vsce or electron-builder as previously implied.
Unreviewed diagnostics still fail the build.

scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs read trailers through git's
%(trailers:key=...) placeholder, which only parses the last paragraph of
a commit message. A release commit that separated Formal-AI-Session and
Formal-AI-Evidence with a blank line therefore exposed only the evidence
trailer, and the resulting "must record both" error failed Auto Release
after the version had already been computed. Trailers are now read from
the full commit body, so placement no longer decides whether a compliant
commit is recognised.

Both suites were confirmed to fail against the pre-fix scripts; the
trailer test reproduces the production error string verbatim. Adds
INSTALL_NODE_DEPENDENCIES_VERBOSE tracing, off by default, and collects
the full investigation under dev/log/issues/796/pulls/797.
commits.messageHeadline: Fix two CI false positives failing the default branch

commits.messageBody:
Filed six upstream issues for the gaps this audit found:

- link-assistant/web-capture#144 (archiver@7 -> deprecated glob@10.5.0)
- rust#97, js#104, python#33 (no top-level permissions: in release.yml)
- rust#98 (cargo install rust-script unretried and unlocked)
- python#34, rust#99 (outdated codecov/codecov-action)

Two corrections to the analysis, both from verifying against the actual
upstream files rather than the earlier draft:

- The rust-script gap exists in the Rust template only, not all three;
  the js and python templates never invoke it. The report was filed
  accordingly.
- A concrete source fix exists for the glob chain: archiver@8.0.0 drops
  archiver-utils entirely for readdir-glob@^3 (minimatch only), so no
  glob remains in its tree. It requires node >= 18, noted upstream.
commits.messageHeadline: Record upstream reports and correct two analysis claims
Add development log for issue #796 PR #797

title: Fix two CI false positives failing the default branch
